hypothesis: If a reinforcement learning generative model maximizes CATS-based pharmacophoric similarity to known estrogen receptor antagonists while penalizing MACCS/MAP4-based structural similarity (alongside a QED term), then it will generate molecules with significantly better ER docking scores, comparable or improved QED/SA, and greater novelty than a QED+QVina baseline because pharmacophore-guided optimization preserves key interaction motifs while enforcing scaffold diversification.
